issues.title: Parameter name mismatch (org vs organization)
issues.attack: Plan uses org=None but codebase uses organization=None. Implementing as-is causes TypeError in all form call sites.
issues.response: Confirmed. All call sites and __init__ use organization=. Plan must match.

issues.title: Step 4 overwrites collision approval workflow + existing M2M conflict
issues.attack: Plan's Step 4 replaces entire project_create body, losing collision/approval/notification flow. Also existing add(request.user) conflicts with form-based assignment.
issues.response: Confirmed. Must preserve both collision branches, call form.save_m2m(), remove old add(request.user), add default fallback.

issues.title: Update view has no implementation step or test
issues.attack: Goal says create/update but steps only cover create. No rules for empty selection on update.
issues.response: Confirmed. Added Step 4.5 for project_update. Policy: empty selection on update is allowed (no default fallback unlike create).

issues.title: Permission premise conflicts with actual code
issues.attack: Design spec says owner-only but code uses @membership_required. Plan doesn't resolve this.
issues.response: Design spec premise overridden by t05 ruling. Plan explicitly states permission is @membership_required unchanged.

issues.title: Tests too narrow scope
issues.attack: Only 2 happy-path tests. No collision path, update path, or negative cases.
issues.response: File placement (test_rbac.py) kept. Test scope expanded to 6 cases covering create/update/consultant/negative.

issues.title: No negative security test cases
issues.attack: No tests for cross-org user PK injection.
issues.response: Added cross-org PK rejection test. distinct() unnecessary due to OneToOneField.

issues.title: CheckboxSelectMultiple scalability concern
issues.attack: Large orgs will have unusable checkbox lists.
issues.response: Valid long-term concern but not plan-level issue. Current deployment has small orgs (<10). Design spec requires this widget.
